steps.title: Understand the two-study logic chain
steps.content: Study 1 established that behavioral similarity between breeds correlates with genetic similarity — especially for chasing. Study 2 found that French bulldogs and bullmastiffs show similar chasing behavior. The question asks what logically follows.

steps.title: Apply the correlation and eliminate wrong inferences
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'their similarities with respect to that behavior could result from a shared aspect of their genetics.': Study 1 says behavioral similarity indicates genetic similarity. Study 2 documents chasing similarity between these two breeds. Applying Study 1's logic to Study 2's finding leads directly here. ✓
  • Choice B — 'individual French bulldogs likely display higher levels of chasing than individual bullmastiffs.': The passage establishes similarity in chasing between the breeds, not a difference in intensity between them. ✗
  • Choice C — 'French bulldogs and bullmastiffs show a greater tendency toward chasing than most other dog breeds do.': No comparison to other breeds is made or implied. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the two breeds will likely become less genetically similar over time.': Nothing in either study suggests future divergence; the passage is about a current correlation. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
